search_queries_used: site:entnet.org tinnitus clinical practice guideline update
candidate_sources_rejected: 2014 AAO-HNS tinnitus guideline as primary current coverage
rejection_reasons: An AAO-HNS tinnitus update is in development and NICE NG155 provides accessible exact current recommendations.
Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
population_applicability: Exact for adults, children, and young people with tinnitus; adult otoscopy support from NG98 is population limited.
setting_applicability: Exact for UK primary, community, and secondary care; not validated for UAE pathways.
UAE_applicability: No exact UAE tinnitus guideline was identified; NICE guidance requires UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG155 and NG98 were opened on 2026-07-12; the AAO-HNS tinnitus update remains in development and was not used.
superseded_check: NG155 remains current official NICE guidance; no published newer AAO-HNS update was used.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped tinnitus terminology, duration, noise, concern, support, management-discussion, and question fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports tinnitus experience, duration, concerns, noise and stress context, hearing association, sleep impact, management discussion, individual preferences, information, and questions.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped unilateral, pulsatile, sudden-hearing, vestibular, neurological, worsening, and referral fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports review of suicide risk, neurological or vestibular symptoms, sudden hearing loss, distress, objective, unilateral, asymmetric, and pulsatile tinnitus, and clinician-arranged referral.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped sleep, concentration, functional, stress, and psychological-impact fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documentation of quality-of-life, sleep, concentration, mental-health, behavioural, and psychological impact.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped hearing assessment and clinician-reviewed audiology fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports audiological assessment, selected tympanometry, neurological or otological context, and differentiated imaging review for pulsatile, unilateral, asymmetric, or otherwise concerning tinnitus.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped adult otoscopy and hearing-screen fields only when hearing loss is relevant.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.

unresolved_source_gaps: Medication history, ear fullness, headache, cervical bruit, generic ENT examination, fixed four-to-six-week timing, previous-record review, and combined medication or noise review remain unsupported.
No UAE-specific exact source was found.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
